mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth above 100k signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high-payroll growth months fall below median baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and rising unemployment indicate supply-side disruptions and weakening demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 2% m/m and UNRATE rises > 0.3pp m/m
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns in co-occurring INDPRO/UNRATE declines exceed 10% in 30%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts above 1.2 million units signal strong residential construction activity that drives demand for equities in the materials and industrials sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high-HOUST periods fall below 0.5% in 40%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly inflation below 2% signals accommodative central bank policy that supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in low-inflation periods show negative correlation with CPIAUCSL (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in co-occurring RSAFS/HOUST peaks fall below 0.3% in 50%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate decline below 4% signals tightening labor market conditions that pressure wages and corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in low-UNRATE periods exceed 1.5% in 30%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production below 100 points indicate weakening supply chain activity that reduces corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in INDPRO declines exceed 5% in 25%+ of cases.
